documents.description: Archive
documents.summary: This document provides information about ForgeCode's ranking and accuracy metrics on TermBench.

documents.title: ForgeCode
documents.description: List of blog posts on ForgeCode blog
documents.summary: This document contains a collection of blog posts from ForgeCode covering AI coding agents, model comparisons, benchmarks, and product updates.

documents.title: ForgeCode
documents.summary: This document appears to be a placeholder or template for cookie settings management, likely related to user privacy and consent configuration.

documents.title: Benchmarks Don't Matter — Until They Do (Part 1)
documents.description: ForgeCode hit 78.4% SOTA on TermBench 2.0 with gemini-3.1-pro-preview. This is the technical account of how we got there: seven failure modes, their fixes, and why the benchmark work generalized across models rather than overfitting to one run.
documents.summary: This document explains how to optimize AI coding agents for benchmark performance by identifying and fixing seven distinct failure modes, including non-interactive mode configuration, tool usage errors, and timing constraints.

documents.title: AI Agent Best Practices: 12 Lessons from AI Pair Programming for Developers
documents.description: Discover field-tested best practices for productive AI-assisted development. Learn 12 crucial lessons from 6 months of daily AI pair programming, covering effective planning, prompt engineering, context management, and common pitfalls to avoid for maximizing developer efficiency.
documents.summary: A practical guide to effective AI pair programming workflows based on 6 months of real-world experience, covering planning strategies, prompt engineering, context management, and best practices for code review and testing.

documents.title: Claude 4 Initial Impressions: A Developer's Review of Anthropic's AI Coding Breakthrough
documents.description: First impressions and in-depth review of Claude 4, highlighting its groundbreaking 72.7% SWE-bench Verified score, real-world coding capabilities, and what this means for the future of AI-assisted software development.
documents.summary: This document analyzes Claude 4's performance on coding benchmarks and real-world development tasks, comparing it to other AI models and demonstrating its capabilities through practical testing scenarios.

documents.title: Claude 4 Opus vs Grok 4: Which Model Dominates Complex Coding Tasks?
documents.description: I pitted Claude 4 Opus against Grok 4 in a series of challenging coding tasks. The results highlight trade-offs in speed, cost, accuracy, and frustration factors that every dev should know.
documents.summary: This document provides a detailed comparison between Grok 4 and Claude 4 Opus AI models, focusing on their performance for complex coding tasks in Rust including bug detection, code refactoring, and tool usage.

documents.title: DeepSeek-R1-0528: A Detailed Review of its AI Coding Performance & Latency
documents.description: A comprehensive review of DeepSeek-R1-0528's AI coding capabilities, architectural innovations, and significant latency challenges via OpenRouter API. Is this open-source LLM ready for your real-time development workflow?
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive review of the DeepSeek-R1-0528 open source reasoning model, analyzing its technical capabilities, performance benchmarks, and practical usability challenges, particularly focusing on latency issues that impact real-world development workflows.

documents.title: ForgeCode v0.98.0: Integrated Authentication and Developer Experience Improvements
documents.description: ForgeCode v0.98.0 release brings browser-based authentication, AI safety limits, and enhanced file operations for AI coding assistants. Streamline your terminal development workflow with improved reliability and developer experience.
documents.summary: This document explains the new features and changes in ForgeCode v0.98.0, including browser-based authentication, safety limits for tool failures, and enhanced file operations like replace-all functionality.

documents.title: ForgeCode Performance RCA: Root Cause Analysis of Quality Degradation on July 12, 2025
documents.description: A detailed root cause analysis of the ForgeCode AI coding assistant's quality degradation incident on July 12, 2025, including the impact of aggressive conversation compaction and steps taken for future prevention and stability improvements.
documents.summary: This document explains how a cost optimization feature that compacted conversation context caused quality degradation in the ForgeCode AI agent, leading to a rollback and plans for improved multi-turn evaluation testing.

documents.title: When Google Sneezes, the Whole World Catches a Cold
documents.description: Deep dive into the IAM failure that took down Google Cloud, cascaded into Cloudflare and Anthropic, and rippled across dozens of internet services.
documents.summary: This document analyzes a major Google Cloud outage caused by IAM authentication failures that cascaded across multiple services including Cloudflare and Anthropic, detailing the timeline, impact, and lessons learned from the incident.

documents.title: Benchmarks Don't Matter — Until They Do (Part 2)
documents.description: ForgeCode now reaches 81.8% on TermBench 2.0 with both GPT 5.4 and Opus 4.6. The interesting part is not the score. It is what we had to change in the agent to make GPT 5.4 behave as reliably as Opus 4.6.
documents.summary: This document explains how ForgeCode improved their agent runtime to achieve 81.8% on TermBench 2.0 by addressing model-specific failure modes and implementing schema optimizations, field ordering changes, and verification enforcement.

documents.title: Graduating from Early Access: New Pricing Tiers Now Available
documents.description: How our explosive early access growth shaped our pricing strategy and what's now available for developers at every scale.
documents.summary: This document announces new pricing tiers for ForgeCode's AI-powered development tools based on early access results and user demand patterns. It explains the free, Pro, and Max plans designed for different types of developer usage.

documents.title: Coding Agents Showdown: VSCode Forks vs. IDE Extensions vs. CLI Agents
documents.description: The AI coding assistant landscape is fragmenting into three distinct ways to integrate AI into your development workflow. Here's an objective analysis of what each approach reveals about the future of software development.
documents.summary: This document analyzes the three distinct approaches for integrating AI into development workflows: VSCode forks, IDE extensions, and CLI agents, comparing their technical architectures, strengths, limitations, and use cases.

documents.title: AI Code Agents: Indexed vs. Non-Indexed Performance for Real-Time Development
documents.description: Explore a benchmark comparison of indexed vs. non-indexed AI coding agents using Apollo 11's guidance computer code. Uncover critical insights into speed, accuracy, and the hidden costs of synchronization in AI-assisted development.
documents.summary: This document presents an experiment comparing AI agent performance on code analysis tasks, testing whether code indexing improves efficiency when analyzing the Apollo Guidance Computer codebase.

documents.title: Kimi K2 vs Grok 4: Which AI Model Codes Better?
documents.description: A deep dive into Kimi K2 and Grok 4 for real-world coding, comparing their performance across bug fixing, feature implementation, tool use, and cost efficiency. See which model stands out and when to choose each for your dev workflow.
documents.summary: This document presents a real-world coding performance comparison between Kimi K2 and Grok 4 AI models, testing their abilities to fix bugs, implement features, and refactor code in a Next.js application.

documents.title: Kimi K2 vs Qwen-3 Coder: Testing Two AI Models on Coding Tasks
documents.description: I tested Kimi K2 and Qwen-3 Coder on 13 Rust development tasks across a 38k-line codebase and 2 Frontend refactor tasks. The results reveal differences in code quality, instruction following, and development capabilities.
documents.summary: This document presents a detailed comparison of Kimi K2 and Qwen-3 Coder AI models based on real-world development tasks in Rust and React, showing performance differences across various coding scenarios.

documents.title: Claude Sonnet 4 vs Kimi K2 vs Gemini 2.5 Pro: Which AI actually ships production code?
documents.description: I ran Claude Sonnet 4, Kimi K2, and Gemini 2.5 Pro on the same Next.js app and measured cost, speed, and whether the code actually shipped without follow-ups.
documents.summary: A comparison of three AI models (Claude Sonnet 4, Kimi K2, and Gemini 2.5 Pro) tested on the same Next.js codebase to evaluate their performance on bug fixes and feature implementations with real-time collaboration using Velt SDK.

documents.title: MCP 2025-06-18 Spec Update: AI Security, Structured Output, and User Elicitation for LLMs
documents.description: Real talk about MCP Spec update (v2025-06-18), including important changes, security implications and what developers should actually care about.
documents.summary: This document explains the new MCP (Model Context Protocol) v2025-06-18 specification update, focusing on security improvements including OAuth 2.0 integration, token binding, and authentication requirements for AI model context protocols.

documents.title: MCP Security Prevention: Practical Strategies for AI Development - Part 2
documents.description: Dive into real-world MCP security vulnerabilities and discover actionable prevention strategies for AI development, focusing on prompt injection, cost-based attacks, and secure credential handling.
documents.summary: This document provides security guidance for protecting AI systems and MCP servers from prompt injection attacks, credential theft, and resource exploitation based on OWASP and NIST frameworks.

documents.title: ForgeCode v0.106.0 Release: Plan Progress Tracking and Reliability Improvements
documents.description: ForgeCode v0.106.0 introduces plan progress tracking for better task management and reliability improvements to enhance your development workflow.
documents.summary: This document explains the new features and improvements in ForgeCode version 0.106.0, including real-time plan progress tracking, VS Code extension functionality, and enhanced reliability features.

documents.title: Claude Sonnet 4 vs Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview: AI Coding Assistant Comparison
documents.description: An in-depth comparison of Claude Sonnet 4 and Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview for AI-assisted coding, evaluating their efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and critical instruction adherence in real-world development workflows.
documents.summary: This document provides a detailed performance comparison between Claude Sonnet 4 and Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview AI models, focusing on execution speed, cost efficiency, and instruction adherence for coding tasks.

documents.title: Simple Over Easy: Architectural Constraints for Maintainable AI-Generated Code
documents.description: Discover how applying Rich Hickey's 'Simple Made Easy' principles can solve the 'AI 90/10 problem', leading to more maintainable and reviewable AI-generated code by constraining architectural choices.
documents.summary: This document explains how to apply Rich Hickey's 'Simple Made Easy' principles to constrain AI code generation, creating architectural patterns that force maintainable code by design.
